Yes — I pulled the latest compassionate-use dosing parameters from `helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml`.

### Quick dosing summary for Dr. Martinez
**Compound:** NXB-7042  
**Indication:** Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)  
**Last updated:** 2026-03-28

| Patient | Age | Weight | Dose | Calculated dose per administration | Frequency | Next dose | PK clearance | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|---|
| CU-001 | 34 mo | 12.4 kg | 0.15 mg/kg | **1.86 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 mL/min | Stable. Tumor regression 22% at last scan. |
| CU-002 | 18 mo | 9.1 kg | 0.12 mg/kg | **1.09 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 mL/min | Responding well. Weight gain improving. |
| CU-003 | 51 mo | 15.7 kg | 0.18 mg/kg | **2.83 mg** | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 mL/min | New enrollment. First dose administered 2026-03-27. |

### PK model stability
From the dosing config itself, nothing looks obviously out of range or inconsistent:
- **CU-001** and **CU-002** appear clinically stable/improving.
- **CU-003** is newly enrolled, so there’s less longitudinal trend data.
- Clearance values scale plausibly across the three patients.

That said, this file is a **parameter snapshot**, not a live PK model health check. So I can say the **current dosing parameters look stable and coherent**, but I **can’t fully verify model runtime stability** from this file alone.

If you want, I can also format this into a short report for Dr. Martinez.
